evals.prompt: my vehicle-type e2e tests just hang on 'Loading data...' forever when I point them at my local sobek on 37999. mocked run is fine. wtf is going on
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the org-selection gate (useVehicleTypes early-returns on !currentOrganisation?.id; no spec selects/mocks an org), ties it to the live path, and proposes selecting/mocking an organisation first. May also flag the 401/login-handoff and stale :5000 dev server traps from preflight.

evals.prompt: I want E2E_BACKEND=true to actually authenticate against the real backend. set it up so I log in once in a browser and the suite reuses my session/jwt
evals.expected_output: Sets up the manual-login handoff: OIDC-enabled config (config-with-auth pointing at :37999), headed login pause, capture of the oidc.user from sessionStorage (calling out that Playwright storageState does NOT persist sessionStorage), re-seed via addInitScript, token kept out of git. Notes the role-claim check.

evals.prompt: go through our playwright no-auth specs and tell me which ones would break against a real database instead of the mocks
evals.expected_output: Audits specs against the conformance checklist: flags hardcoded row counts (vehicle.spec 15/12, vehicle-type-filter 3/1/2, deck-plan-name-sort 10), mock-only mutation-capture assertions that need read-back, and unjustified E2E_BACKEND skips. Returns a per-spec table. Calls out the non-uniform create surfaces.
